text: Large language models, or LLMs, are a type of artificial intelligence that can understand and generate human-like text. They work by processing vast amounts of written material, which is called training data. This data includes books, articles, and websites, helping the model learn patterns in language. When you give an LLM a prompt—a question or instruction—it uses these patterns to predict what words should come next. This is known as prompt engineering, where the way you ask can affect the answer. LLMs are very capable at tasks like writing stories or answering questions, but they have limits. They can make mistakes, lack true understanding, and sometimes generate false information based on what they learned.
